title: Deliciously Poached Korean Fish Bowl
description: A comforting and flavorful korean dish featuring fresh fish poached in a sweet and spicy sauce, served over a bed of steaming white rice.

ingredients.name: white rice
ingredients.notes: Short-grain rice for authentic korean texture

ingredients.name: gochujang
ingredients.notes: Korean chili paste for spicy kick

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium soy sauce for balanced flavor

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince and sauté for depth of flavor

instructions: Rinse the salmon fillets under cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
In a medium saucepan, combine gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and 1/4 cup water.
Whisk until smooth and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and poach the salmon fillets for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, cook the white rice according to package instructions.
In a small skillet, toast the sesame seeds over low heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant and lightly browned.
Once the fish is done, remove it from the sauce and place on a plate.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Simmer the sauce over low heat until reduced and slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
To assemble the bowls, place a scoop of cooked white rice on the bottom, followed by a piece of poached salmon.
Drizzle the reduced sauce over the top and garnish with toasted sesame seeds, chopped cilantro, and thinly sliced green onions.
